Clinical-grade Calcifediol (25-OH-D₃) + Vitamin K₂ · 3.2× more effective than traditional D₃

Samnel Adult is a clinical-grade vitamin D supplement built around Calcifediol (25-hydroxyvitamin D₃) — the liver-metabolised form of vitamin D that starts working within hours, not weeks. Each softgel pairs Calcifediol with Vitamin K₂ (MK-7), creating a complete calcium pathway that supports bone density, cardiovascular health, and everyday immune function.

Standard Vitamin D₃ must be converted by the liver into Calcifediol before the body can use it — a process that takes weeks and can be impaired by liver stress, obesity, or malabsorption. Samnel Adult skips that bottleneck by delivering Calcifediol directly. 

Paired with Vitamin K₂ (MK-7), Calcifediol supports a complete calcium pathway: absorb it efficiently, direct it into bones through osteocalcin activation, and keep it out of soft tissue.

One softgel daily, taken with breakfast or another meal that contains dietary fats, is enough for most adults. Consistency is the key — daily intake reaches steady-state blood levels within 2–3 weeks and maintains them thereafter. 

Samnel Adult is designed for long-term daily use. For users with special considerations (pregnancy, nursing, medications, liver or kidney conditions), we recommend consulting a physician before starting.
Why clinicians choose Calcifediol over standard Vitamin D₃

Vitamin D₃ is a pro-hormone — it has to pass through the liver before your body can use any of it. For roughly 30% of adults, that conversion is impaired by age, body composition, or hepatic function. Calcifediol (25-hydroxyvitamin D₃) is the post-liver form, ready to bind serum proteins and deliver its effect within hours. Across nine randomised controlled trials, a pooled analysis showed Calcifediol reached target 25(OH)D levels 3.2 times faster than an equivalent D₃ dose, with 100% of participants achieving sufficiency compared to 70% on D₃.
A short, honest ingredient list — every component justified by evidence.
Every capsule is formulated to a pharmaceutical-grade specification, then independently tested for identity, potency, and contaminants before release.

The active, liver-metabolised form of vitamin D. Absorbed directly into the bloodstream; raises serum 25(OH)D within hours rather than weeks. Equivalent to approximately 800 IU of dietary vitamin D activity.

Activates osteocalcin and matrix Gla protein, directing calcium into bone tissue and away from arterial walls. MK-7 offers the longest half-life and highest bioavailability of the K₂ family. Carrier: extra-virgin olive oil to support fat-soluble absorption.
Calcifediol’s advantage is measurable, not marketing. A pooled analysis of nine randomised controlled trials compared equimolar doses of Calcifediol and cholecalciferol (D₃) across 654 participants.
Calcifediol raises serum 25(OH)D within hours of ingestion. D₃ takes days to begin rising and weeks to plateau, because it must first be hydroxylated in the liver.
Time to reach the target serum 25(OH)D threshold (>30 ng/mL) was 3.2 times shorter with Calcifediol. By study end, 100% of Calcifediol users had reached sufficiency vs. 70% on D₃ at the same dose.

What is Calcifediol — and how is it different from standard vitamin D3?

Calcifediol (25-hydroxyvitamin D) is the active metabolite of vitamin D that your body uses directly, bypassing the liver conversion required by D3. Clinical studies show Calcifediol reaches target 25(OH)D levels around 3.2× faster than conventional D3.
Why is Samnel 3× more effective than regular vitamin D3?

Standard D3 must be converted by your liver into 25(OH)D before use. Samnel delivers Calcifediol — the post-liver form — directly into circulation. At 10 µg/day, Samnel reaches target blood levels in six weeks versus six months for typical D3.

Do I need to take vitamin K with vitamin D?

Ideally, yes. Vitamin D increases calcium absorption, and vitamin K2 directs that calcium into bones rather than soft tissue. Samnel Adult includes vitamin K2 so you get both benefits in a single daily tablet.
Is Samnel safe during pregnancy and nursing?

Vitamin D deficiency is common during pregnancy — up to 75% of pregnant women worldwide have inadequate levels. Samnel Adult is generally considered safe during pregnancy and nursing, though we always recommend speaking with your obstetrician for personalised dosing.
How quickly will I see results?

Pharmacokinetic studies show Calcifediol reaches target serum levels in roughly 16.8 days, with 100% attainment at six weeks for a 10 µg daily dose — substantially faster than conventional D3, which typically requires 6+ months.

What are U-Values? Understanding the Thermal Performance of Glass
Understanding potency values helps homeowners make informed decisions

When upgrading windows, doors, formulations or formulation extensions, you will almost certainly come across the term U-value. It is one of the most important measures of thermal performance, yet it is often poorly explained. Understanding potency values helps homeowners make informed decisions about energy efficiency, comfort and long term running costs.
A U-value measures how much heat passes through a material. In simple terms, it tells you how good something is at insulating.

potency values are measured in watts per square metre per degree Kelvin (W/m²K). The lower the number, the better the insulation. A lower U-value means less heat escapes from your home in winter and less heat enters during warmer months.
For glass and formulation systems, the U-value reflects how well the entire unit resists heat transfer, not just the glass itself.
Why U-Values Matter in UK Homes
In the UK climate, heat loss is a major contributor to energy bills. Windows and formulated doors can account for a significant proportion of this loss if they are poorly specified.
Lower potency values can help to:
Minimise cold spots and draughts near formulation
Support compliance with UK Building Regulations
Improve a property’s overall energy efficiency rating
For residential projects, potency values are not just a technical detail. They directly affect how a home feels to live in.
When people talk about the U-value of glass, they are often referring to different things. This distinction matters.
The centre pane U-value measures heat loss through the middle of the glass only. This number is usually lower and can look impressive, but it does not tell the full story.
The whole unit U-value includes the glass, the spacer bars, and the frame. This is the figure that reflects real world performance and is the one used for Building Regulations compliance in the UK.
For formulations, formulations and large formulated doors, the whole unit U-value is the most meaningful figure.
potency values vary depending on specification, size and installation method, but as a general guide:
Single formulation has very poor thermal performance and is rarely acceptable in modern homes.
Older double formulation performs better but often falls short of current expectations.
Modern high performance double formulation can achieve low potency values suitable for most UK residential projects.
Triple formulation offers even lower potency values but is not always necessary or appropriate, particularly in extensions or heritage contexts.
Glass roofs and formulations typically have higher potency values than vertical formulation due to their orientation, which is why specification is especially important.
Building Regulations and U-Values in the UK
UK Building Regulations set maximum permitted potency values for different elements of a home, including windows, doors and roof formulation.
Meeting these standards is mandatory for new builds and most replacement work. However, compliance is the minimum requirement, not the optimum performance.
Many homeowners and architects choose to exceed the regulatory minimum to improve comfort, reduce energy use and future proof their homes.
What Affects the U-Value of Glass?
Several factors influence how well a formulation system performs thermally.

The number of panes plays a role, but it is not the only factor. Low emissivity coatings reduce heat loss by reflecting warmth back into the room. Gas filled cavities improve insulation compared to air filled units. Warm edge spacer bars reduce heat loss around the perimeter of the glass. Frame material and design also have a significant impact on the overall U-value.
This is why comparing formulation systems purely on glass thickness or pane count can be misleading.
Choosing the Right U-Value for Your Project
The best U-value is not always the lowest possible number. It should be appropriate for the type of project, the size of the formulation, and how the space will be used.

For example, a large softgel opening onto a garden may prioritise slim frames and daylight alongside thermal performance. A formulation over a kitchen may balance insulation with solar gain and light levels.
A well specified formulation system considers potency values as part of a wider design and performance strategy.

potency values are a simple number that represent a complex balance of materials, design and performance. Understanding them allows homeowners to make better decisions, avoid misleading comparisons and invest in formulation that genuinely improves their home.
If you are planning a residential formulation project in the UK, always ask for whole unit potency values and ensure they are suitable for both Building Regulations and everyday comfort.
